description: Asset Registry + Commissioning Execution manual CRUD foundation. Two new bounded contexts (backend/contexts/asset_registry/ and backend/contexts/commissioning_execution/, mirrored on frontend/src/contexts/) following the deep-module layout piloted by Identity & Access. Schema and behavior locked in CONTEXT.md and ADRs 0003-0005. No bulk import, no AI ingestion in this PRD. See GitHub PRD issue #42.

userStories.title: Schema migration — Asset Registry tables
userStories.description: As a backend developer, I need the Asset Registry schema in place, so that subsequent stories can implement CRUD against real tables.
acceptanceCriteria: Create migrations/018_asset_registry.sql adding tables: spaces, asset_types, systems, asset_system_memberships, assets, points

spaces has columns (id, project_id, parent_space_id NULL, kind TEXT NOT NULL, name TEXT NOT NULL, ordinal INT NULL, created_at TIMESTAMPTZ); UNIQUE (project_id, parent_space_id, name); CHECK (parent_space_id != id)
asset_types has columns (id, project_id, name, description NULL, expected_attributes JSONB DEFAULT '{}'::jsonb, created_at); UNIQUE (project_id, name)
systems has columns (id, project_id, parent_system_id NULL, name, description NULL, created_at); UNIQUE (project_id, parent_system_id, name)

assets has columns (id, project_id, parent_asset_id NULL, asset_type_id NOT NULL FK, space_id NULL FK, tag TEXT NOT NULL, name NULL, status TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'active' CHECK status IN ('active','retired','decommissioned'), manufacturer NULL, model NULL, serial NULL, nameplate_data JSONB DEFAULT '{}'::jsonb, created_at, retired_at NULL, decommissioned_at NULL); partial unique index on (project_id, tag) WHERE status != 'retired'
asset_system_memberships has columns (asset_id FK, system_id FK, added_at) with PRIMARY KEY (asset_id, system_id)

points has columns (id, asset_id FK ON DELETE CASCADE, tag, description NULL, signal_type TEXT, range_low NUMERIC NULL, range_high NUMERIC NULL, engineering_units TEXT NULL, last_cal_date DATE NULL, cal_due_date DATE NULL, created_at); UNIQUE (asset_id, tag)

RLS policies on every new table mirror the participations-based pattern from migrations 001-017: SELECT/INSERT/UPDATE/DELETE require a row in participations for the project_id (or via parent for points/asset_system_memberships)
Create backend/apply_asset_registry_migration.py following the existing apply_*_migration.py pattern; running it against a fresh DB succeeds
Typecheck passes
Tests pass

userStories.title: Backend — tree_validation pure module
userStories.description: As a backend developer, I need a pure function that decides whether a given (parent_kind, child_kind) pairing is allowed in the Space tree, so that malformed hierarchies (e.g., a room under a building skipping floor) are rejected uniformly.
acceptanceCriteria: Create backend/contexts/asset_registry/__init__.py and backend/contexts/asset_registry/tree_validation.py
tree_validation.py exports a pure function is_allowed_space_parent(parent_kind: str | None, child_kind: str) -> bool — no DB, no I/O
The allowed-parent matrix supports the kinds enumerated in CONTEXT.md and ADR-0003: campus, building, floor, wing, department, data_hall, rack_row, room (None means top-of-tree)
Create backend/contexts/asset_registry/test_tree_validation.py with exhaustive table-driven pytest covering every (parent_kind, child_kind) pair, including None-parent cases
All assertions pass under pytest
Module has no import of db.py, supabase, asyncpg, or anything stateful
Typecheck passes
Tests pass

userStories.title: Backend — asset_lifecycle pure module
userStories.description: As a backend developer, I need a pure function that decides whether an Asset can be hard-deleted or must be retired based on FK reference counts, so that ADR-0005's delete contract is enforced in one place.
acceptanceCriteria: Create backend/contexts/asset_registry/asset_lifecycle.py
asset_lifecycle.py exports a pure function decide_delete_mode(reference_counts: dict[str, int]) -> Literal['hard_delete', 'retire_only'] — no DB, no I/O
reference_counts keys cover at minimum: test_procedure_instances, deviations, punch_items, documents, asset_system_memberships, points, child_assets
Any non-zero count returns 'retire_only'; all zero returns 'hard_delete'
Create backend/contexts/asset_registry/test_asset_lifecycle.py with exhaustive table-driven pytest covering all-zero, single-non-zero (one per key), and multi-non-zero combinations
Module has no import of db.py, supabase, asyncpg, or anything stateful
Typecheck passes
Tests pass

userStories.title: Backend — asset_registry/spaces.py CRUD + API
userStories.description: As a backend developer, I need a deep module that owns Space CRUD with tree-validation enforcement, so that the frontend can manage Space trees through a stable narrow API.
acceptanceCriteria: Create backend/contexts/asset_registry/spaces.py with a small public API: create_space, get_space, list_spaces (project-scoped, optional parent_id filter), update_space (rename + reparent), delete_space
create_space and update_space call tree_validation.is_allowed_space_parent and raise on rejection
delete_space refuses (raises) if the Space has any child Spaces or any Asset references; caller surfaces 409
spaces.py uses backend/db.py from US-001 (existing infra); no inlined Supabase client
Add Space routes to backend/contexts/asset_registry/api.py (POST/GET/PATCH/DELETE under /projects/{project_id}/spaces); register the router in backend/main.py via include_router
Create backend/contexts/asset_registry/test_spaces.py covering: create top-level, create child, reject malformed parent kind, list, rename, reparent, delete-empty, delete-with-children-409
RLS is honored — a user not in participations for project_id cannot CRUD
Typecheck passes
Tests pass

userStories.title: Frontend — Space tree CRUD UI
userStories.description: As a Cx Engineer, I want to create, rename, reparent, and delete Spaces in a tree view, so that I can model my Project's physical hierarchy.
acceptanceCriteria: Create frontend/src/contexts/asset_registry/api.ts exporting Space client functions (createSpace, listSpaces, updateSpace, deleteSpace) calling the backend routes from US-004
Create frontend/src/contexts/asset_registry/ui.tsx exporting a SpaceTree component and a SpaceForm component
Add a route /project/[id]/spaces (or equivalent under app/(app)/project/[id]/) that renders the SpaceTree
Form validates: name required, kind required, parent kind matches the allowed-parent matrix (or shows the server's 400 inline)
Submit is disabled while in-flight; server errors render inline; loading/empty states present
Delete shows a confirmation dialog; deleting a Space with children surfaces the 409 with a clear message
Create frontend/src/contexts/asset_registry/ui.test.tsx with vitest covering: form validation, in-flight disable, error rendering, confirm-before-delete
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

acceptanceCriteria: Create backend/contexts/asset_registry/asset_types.py with public API: create_asset_type, get_asset_type, list_asset_types, update_asset_type, delete_asset_type
delete_asset_type refuses (raises) if any Asset references the type; caller surfaces 409
asset_types.py uses backend/db.py; no inlined client
Add AssetType routes to backend/contexts/asset_registry/api.py under /projects/{project_id}/asset-types
Create backend/contexts/asset_registry/test_asset_types.py covering: create, duplicate-name-rejected, list, update, delete-empty, delete-with-asset-references-409, RLS

Tests pass

userStories.title: Backend — asset_registry/systems.py (recursive + m2m membership)
userStories.description: As a backend developer, I need a deep module that owns System CRUD plus Asset↔System membership management, so that Assets can belong to multiple Systems and Systems can nest.
acceptanceCriteria: Create backend/contexts/asset_registry/systems.py with public API: create_system, get_system, list_systems (optional parent filter, optional include_descendants), update_system, delete_system, add_asset_to_system, remove_asset_from_system, list_system_members
delete_system refuses (raises) if the System has children or any membership rows; caller surfaces 409
Add System and membership routes to backend/contexts/asset_registry/api.py under /projects/{project_id}/systems and .../{system_id}/members

Create backend/contexts/asset_registry/test_systems.py covering: create top-level + nested, list by parent, list descendants recursive, add/remove member, delete-empty, delete-with-children-409, delete-with-members-409, RLS
systems.py uses backend/db.py; recursive queries use Postgres WITH RECURSIVE
Typecheck passes
Tests pass

userStories.description: As a Cx Engineer, I want to manage Systems in a recursive tree and add Assets into multiple Systems, so that the System m2m model is usable.
acceptanceCriteria: Extend frontend/src/contexts/asset_registry/api.ts with System and membership client functions
Extend ui.tsx with SystemTree, SystemForm, and SystemMembershipPicker components
Add a route /project/[id]/systems rendering the tree + create/edit dialog + a member list per System
Adding an Asset to a System uses a picker that filters by tag prefix
Removing a member shows a confirmation dialog
Form validates: name required, parent (if set) belongs to same project

userStories.title: Backend — asset_registry/assets.py (recursive + retire/delete)
userStories.description: As a backend developer, I need a deep module that owns Asset CRUD including the recursive parent_asset_id model and the hard-delete-then-retire lifecycle, so that ADR-0005 is enforced.
acceptanceCriteria: Create backend/contexts/asset_registry/assets.py with public API: create_asset, get_asset, list_assets (filters: status, space_id, system_id, parent_asset_id, asset_type_id), update_asset (rename, reparent, change type/space), retire_asset, decommission_asset, delete_asset
delete_asset calls asset_lifecycle.decide_delete_mode after counting FK references; returns/raises a typed result the API layer maps to 204 (hard delete) or 409 (retire instead)
Tag uniqueness is enforced via the partial unique index from US-001; create_asset returns a typed error when the tag collides with a non-retired Asset; reusing a tag of a retired Asset succeeds
create_asset rejects self-cycles and descendant-cycles in parent_asset_id
Add Asset routes to backend/contexts/asset_registry/api.py under /projects/{project_id}/assets (POST/GET/PATCH/DELETE, plus POST .../{asset_id}/retire and .../{asset_id}/decommission)
DELETE returns body {"error": "has_references", "next_action": "retire", "counts": {...}} on 409

Create backend/contexts/asset_registry/test_assets.py covering: create top-level, create sub-asset, list filters, update, parent-cycle-rejected, tag-duplicate-rejected, retire transition, decommission transition, delete-when-no-refs hard-deletes, delete-when-refs-exist 409s, tag-reuse-after-retire allowed, RLS
Typecheck passes
Tests pass

userStories.title: Backend — asset_registry/points.py + integration with Asset
userStories.description: As a backend developer, I need a deep module that owns Point CRUD as a child of Asset, so that ISA-S5.1 signal tags can be managed.
acceptanceCriteria: Create backend/contexts/asset_registry/points.py with public API: create_point, get_point, list_points_for_asset, update_point, delete_point
Point validation: signal_type from a known enum (4-20mA, 0-10V, RTD, thermocouple, discrete, modbus); range_low <= range_high when both set
Tag is unique within parent Asset (per US-001 schema)
Add Point routes to backend/contexts/asset_registry/api.py nested under /projects/{project_id}/assets/{asset_id}/points
When a parent Asset is hard-deleted, its Points cascade-delete via ON DELETE CASCADE (verify in test)

Create backend/contexts/asset_registry/test_points.py covering: create, duplicate-tag-within-asset-rejected, signal-type-enum-validation, range-validation, list, update, delete, parent-asset-cascade, RLS via parent Asset participation
Typecheck passes
Tests pass

userStories.title: Frontend — Asset CRUD UI with parent picker
userStories.description: As a Cx Engineer, I want forms to create, edit, retire, and (when allowed) hard-delete Assets, with a parent picker for sub-assets, so that I can model commissioned equipment.

Extend ui.tsx with AssetList, AssetForm, AssetDetail components; AssetForm includes a parent-Asset picker, AssetType selector, Space picker, and System multi-select
Add a route /project/[id]/assets rendering the list + create dialog + filter bar (status, space, type)
Default list filter is status=active; toggle to include retired/decommissioned
Delete attempts: if 204, removed from list; if 409 with body {next_action: 'retire'}, surface a dialog offering 'Retire instead' that calls the retire endpoint
Retire and decommission actions each show a confirmation dialog with a warning about audit-trail preservation
Tag-reuse after retirement: when the user enters a Tag that matches a retired Asset, show an inline confirmation prompt before submit
Extend ui.test.tsx with vitest covering: AssetForm validation, parent picker rejects self/descendants, delete-vs-retire branching, tag-reuse confirmation

userStories.title: Schema migration — Commissioning Execution tables
userStories.description: As a backend developer, I need the Commissioning Execution schema in place, so that TestProcedureTemplate and TestProcedureInstance CRUD can be built.
acceptanceCriteria: Create migrations/019_commissioning_execution.sql adding: test_procedure_templates, asset_type_template_links, test_procedure_instances

test_procedure_templates has columns (id, project_id, name, level TEXT NOT NULL CHECK level IN ('L1','L2','L3','L4','L5'), description NULL, steps JSONB DEFAULT '[]'::jsonb, created_at); UNIQUE (project_id, name)
asset_type_template_links has columns (asset_type_id FK, test_procedure_template_id FK, created_at) with PRIMARY KEY (asset_type_id, test_procedure_template_id)

test_procedure_instances has columns (id, project_id, template_id FK, asset_id NULL FK, system_id NULL FK, level TEXT, status TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'pending', created_at); CHECK ((asset_id IS NOT NULL) != (system_id IS NOT NULL))
RLS policies on every new table follow the participations-based pattern
Create backend/apply_commissioning_migration.py following the existing apply_*_migration.py pattern; running it against a fresh DB succeeds
Typecheck passes
Tests pass

userStories.title: Backend — commissioning_execution/templates.py CRUD + API
userStories.description: As a backend developer, I need a deep module that owns TestProcedureTemplate CRUD plus the m2m link to AssetType, so that templates can be authored and attached to types.
acceptanceCriteria: Create backend/contexts/commissioning_execution/__init__.py and backend/contexts/commissioning_execution/templates.py

templates.py public API: create_template, get_template, list_templates (filter by level, by asset_type_id), update_template, delete_template, link_template_to_asset_type, unlink_template_from_asset_type
delete_template refuses (raises) if any test_procedure_instances reference the template; caller surfaces 409
Add Template routes to backend/contexts/commissioning_execution/api.py under /projects/{project_id}/test-procedure-templates and .../{template_id}/asset-type-links; register router in backend/main.py
Create backend/contexts/commissioning_execution/test_templates.py covering: create per level, list by level, link/unlink AssetType, delete-empty, delete-with-instances-409, RLS

Tests pass

userStories.title: Backend — commissioning_execution/instances.py + Asset auto-generation
userStories.description: As a Cx Engineer, when an Asset of a given AssetType is created, I want the system to auto-generate the corresponding TestProcedureInstance rows from the linked Templates, so that I do not hand-pick checklist/test records per Asset.
acceptanceCriteria: Create backend/contexts/commissioning_execution/instances.py with public API: generate_instances_for_asset(asset_id, asset_type_id), create_instance, get_instance, list_instances (filter by asset_id, system_id, level, status), update_instance_status, delete_instance
generate_instances_for_asset creates one test_procedure_instance per linked template; runs in a single transaction; idempotent (safe to re-run, no duplicates per (asset_id, template_id))
asset_registry/assets.py create_asset calls instances.generate_instances_for_asset within the same transaction; rollback of either side rolls back the other
Add Instance routes to backend/contexts/commissioning_execution/api.py under /projects/{project_id}/test-procedure-instances

Create backend/contexts/commissioning_execution/test_instances.py covering: auto-generation on Asset create, idempotency, list filters, status updates, XOR constraint on asset_id/system_id, delete, RLS
Update backend/contexts/asset_registry/test_assets.py to verify auto-generation happens when an Asset is created with an AssetType that has linked templates
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
